fact: April 10 is exactly 100 days into the year! That means there are exactly 265 days left until New Year's Eve.
In the Northern Hemisphere, cherry blossom trees are typically in full bloom around this time, dropping pink petals like nature's confetti machine!
Robins in northern climates are usually extra active around April 10, building nests and showing off their bright red bellies to attract mates!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west around April 10 - it's like nature's perfect compass!
Earthworms are typically having their busiest season right now in temperate regions because the soil is finally warm and moist enough for them to wiggle around freely.
April 10 falls in Aries season, which runs from March 21 to April 19 in the zodiac calendar.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are often turning brilliant orange and red around this time because it's actually autumn there - the opposite of spring!
Dandelions are typically blooming everywhere in northern countries around this date, and each fluffy seed head contains about 150-200 seeds ready to fly away!
Maple trees in cold climates like Canada are usually pumping sugar-rich sap up their trunks around this time, which is why maple syrup season often peaks in early spring!
April 10 sits exactly 20 days after the spring equinox, which means daylight is increasing by about 2-3 minutes every single day in the Northern Hemisphere.
Bears and other hibernating animals in northern regions are typically fully awake and very hungry by now - they often lose up to 30% of their body weight during their long winter naps!
